''[[Spider-Man versus Sandman]]'' |actor = [[Tobey Maguire]] |voice actor = [[Tobey Maguire]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|The night Ben died, I hunted down the man who I thought did it. I wanted him dead. I got what I wanted. It didn't make it better. It took me a long time to... learn to get through that darkness.|Peter Parker to [[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Spider-Man|Spider-Man]]|wikipedia:Spider-Man: No Way Home}} '''Peter Benjamin Parker''' is a photographer at ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' who gained his proportionate powers and abilities of a [[Spiders|spider]], becoming a superhero known as '''Spider-Man'''. [[2002|During his teenage years]], Peter got bitten by a genetically engineered spider, gaining his powers at that point. Initially deciding on using them for money, he then realized he should use his powers to protect others following his uncle [[Ben Parker]]'s death. Afterwards, Spider-Man came into conflict with the [[Green Goblin]], who was the villainous alter ego of [[Norman Osborn]], the father of Peter's best friend [[Harry Osborn]]. Spider-Man defeated him but Norman died in the process. [[2004|Two years later]], Peter met [[Otto Octavius]], who was building a fusion based reactor, which turned into a failure. Otto started getting controlled by the tentacles becoming the villain [[Doctor Octopus]]. During this time, a vengeful Harry found out Peter was Spider-Man, much to his surprise as he believed Spider-Man responsible for his father's death. Spider-Man defeated Otto and convinced Octavius to to stop the fusion reactor from happening, but Octavius died in the process. Following this incident, Peter and his childhood love [[Mary Jane Watson]] became a couple. [[2005|After a year]] following [[Battle at the Fusion Reactor|these events]], Peter came into conflict with Harry, but Harry suffered from amnesia as a result of their fight, forgetting everything between him and Spider-Man. While this happened, Peter got into a rivalry with [[Eddie Brock]] over a staff job for the ''Bugle''. It was during this time Peter, alongside his aunt [[May Parker]], found out the true killer of Ben, Flint Marko, also known as [[Sandman]]. Enraged, Spider-Man bonded with an unknown [[Venom|substance]], and targeted the Sandman. While under Venom's influence, he found out Brock had taken fake photos to prove Spider-Man was evil and got the staff job for himself. Eventually, after discovering the true nature of the [[Symbiote|symbiote]], he got rid of it, but it bonded to a vengeful Eddie Brock known as [[Venom]]. Spider-Man then faced the combined forces of Eddie Brock and Sandman with a reformed Harry, with the latter being killed by Eddie. Following Eddie's defeat and death, Peter made peace with the Sandman, with the latter regretfully admitting that killing his Uncle was an accident. Peter and Harry then reconciled their friendship, before Peter and MJ tearfully witnessed Harry's death. After this, Peter and MJ rekindled their love for each other, sharing a dance. Despite the complications due to Peter's double life, he and Mary Jane Watson finally became a stable couple by mending their differences. Peter additionally, became a seasoned and mature hero due to the experience he has accumulated all these years. 19 years after Harry's death, Peter would end up in an [https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe alternate reality], ending up stranded in an alternate version of [[New York City]]. Due to these events, Peter ends up meeting two younger [https://amazingspiderman.fandom.com/wiki/Spider-Man alternate] [https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Spider-Man versions] of himself, Which he had to help after realizing that alternate versions of his former villains had also made their way into young Peter's home universe. Parker then designed an antiserum for the Green Goblin to use in a later battle at the Statue of Liberty. Peter along with variants of himself and a redeemed alternate version of [[Doctor Octopus/Variant Doctor Octopus|Octavius]] fight Sandman, and alternate versions of [[Green Goblin/Variant Green Goblin|Green Goblin]], [https://amazingspiderman.fandom.com/wiki/Electro/Variant_Electro Electro] and [https://amazingspiderman.fandom.com/wiki/Lizard/Variant_Electro Lizard] to cure their afflictions. When the native universe's Spider-Man from this universe attempted to kill the Green Goblin for murdering his Aunt May, Spider-Man forced him to retreat. After all of his adversaries had been cured, Spider-Man embraced alternate versions of him before that universe Doctor Strange returned him to his home universe. == Biography == ===Early life=== ==== Growing Up In New York ==== [[File:Peter's childhood comic.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|A young Peter with [[Ben Parker]]]] {{Quote|You know, you were about 6 years old when MJ's family moved in next door. When she got out of the car, and you saw her for the first time you grabbed me and said, 'Aunt May, aunt May, is that an angel?'|[[May Parker]] to Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} Peter Parker was born in Wisconsin in [[Before 21st Century|August, 1983]]. After Peter's [[Richard Parker|par]][[Mary Parker|ents]] died in a plane crash, an orphaned four year old Parker moved into a house in Forest Hills, New York City with [[Ben Parker]] and [[May Parker]], both of whom were his aunt and uncle. He shared a very close relationship with his uncle and aunt, viewing them as his parents. When he was six, he met [[Mary Jane Watson]], his next-door neighbor, whom he initially believed to be an angel. Parker also used to be a fan of comics, especially those featuring ''Captain America'' and the ''Red Skull,'' and often used to read them.''[[Spider-Man (novel)]]'' Chapter 1 ====School Life==== {{Quote|Harry tells me you're quite the science whiz. Y'know, I'm something of a scientist myself. "
"I read all your research on nanotechnology. Really Brilliant."
"You understood it?"
"Yes, I wrote a paper on it.|[[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} Parker attended [[Midtown High|Midtown High School]] in [[Before 21st Century|1998]], and become a brilliant yet socially inept student. He attended school with MJ and [[Flash Thompson]], the latter of whom was a bully to him. Later on in his life, he met [[Harry Osborn]], the son of renowned [[Oscorp]] founder [[Norman Osborn]]. He and Harry shared a best friend relationship with Harry, getting him through high school academically, while Harry protected him from bullies such as Flash. At some point during high school, Parker idolized both [[Otto Octavius]][[Spider-Man 2|''Spider-Man 2'']] and [[Norman Osborn]], and wrote a paper on the latter's research on nanotechnology which he understood and praised. ===Graduating from High School=== ====School Trip==== [[File:Peter Parker running.webp|thumb|250x250px|Peter running after the bus]] {{Quote|Some spiders change colors to blend into their environment. It's a defense mechanism."
"Peter, what makes you think I'd want to know that?"
"Who wouldn't?|Peter Parker and [[New Goblin|Harry Osborn]]|Spider-Man (film)}} During his senior year of high school in the year [[2002]], weeks before graduation, Peter and his peers were set to go to a science trip held at the genetics laboratory at [[Columbia University]]. However, he missed the bus. So, starting from Woodhaven Boulevard, chased down the school bus, which didn't stop for him, rather everyone on the bus, including the driver, made fun of him. Eventually, the bus stopped for him after MJ pointed out he had been running for a really long time. As soon as he got on the bus, many people refused to save him a seat, including [[Liz Allan]], who told him to not think about it. As soon as he saw MJ next to Flash, he got distracted, causing him to be tripped over by one of Flash's cronies. [[File:Peter-and-harry.webp|left|thumb|250px|Peter meets [[Harry Osborn|Harry]]'s [[Green Goblin|father]] for the first time]] After arriving, Parker met Harry Osborn in front of Columbia University's Genetic Labs where the presentation was being held, however, Harry's bag was returned by Norman as Harry forgot it. Seeing that Peter and Norman were both there, Harry introduced them to each other. Norman then revealed that Harry had spoke big of him, which led Peter to reveal he wrote a paper on Norman's nanotech research. They then complimented each other, before saying goodbye. After their conversation, Harry joked about Norman potentially wanting to adopt Peter. [[File:PeterLooksOverSpiderBite.jpg|thumb|262x262px|Parker is bitten by a [[Spiders|spider]]]] During the science trip, Peter decided to take pictures for the school's newspaper, but he was nudged by Flash's friend, making the picture look bad. He then proceeded to take more pictures of the spiders that were being presented, but he was nudged more. Harry then tried to defend Peter, however the teacher caught them talking and told them that if he caught someone else talking they would fail the semester. As the two continued on, Peter explained to Harry about spiders changing color, which Harry took no interest in. Then, after seeing MJ looking at the spiders, Harry dared Peter to go up and talk to her. Peter refused, so Harry instead went and talked, getting into trouble by the teacher. Peter then asked MJ to take a picture with the spiders, which MJ did. However, one of the escaped genetically engineered super spiders from the display crawled onto Peter's hand, biting him. After this, Peter began to feel sick and left. ====Changing==== [[File:Peter muscles.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter discovers his body has changed from the spider bite]] {{Quote|Are you all right?"
"I'm fine."
"Any better this morning? Any change?"
"Change? Yep. Big change.|[[May Parker]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} That same day, Peter returned home, which his aunt pointed out was just in time for dinner. However, Peter refused, instead opting to take a nap. As he went to the room, he collapsed, with his DNA beginning to change as a result of the bite he took from the experimented spider. He then began to experience changes in his body, while he was unconscious. That morning, however, Parker woke up feeling perfectly fine. As he put on his glasses, he realized that he didn't need them anymore as it blurred his sight, and he then noticed his shape was much more stronger. When May asked if he was alright and if he was going through a change, he then replied he was going through a big change. He then rushed downstairs, getting his bag and leaving without eating, but not before Ben reminded him that they were painting the kitchen that night. ====Fight with Flash Thompson==== [[File:Peter with Mary Jane.webp|thumb|250px|Peter catches Mary Jane's food tray in seconds]] {{Quote|Jesus, Parker, you are a freak.|[[List of Minor Characters#Spider-Man|Flash's Crony]] to Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} As soon as it got to lunchtime that day, Peter sat by himself, eating lunch. While eating, he saw MJ walking and began to gaze at her in admiration. But as she walked past, she began to fall over a puddle. Peter, with somehow an incredible sense, he was able to catch MJ, and all her food. MJ then walked away as Parker sat down to eat. He then noticed a fork attached to his hand, and when he tried to get it off, he accidentally discovered he could shoot organic webbing, catching onto someone else's lunch. Parker then tried to get the web off him but hit Flash in the process. After realizing this Parker then walked away. [[File:Dodge.png|left|thumb|250x250px|Parker dodges [[Flash Thompson]]'s punch]] While he got to his locker, he sensed Flash about to punch him, so he dodged it. After dodging a few of his attacks, he got shocked realizing how quick he was to dodge them. He then dodged an attack from Flash's friend, whom Flash pushed out the way. He then broke Flash's arm, before punching him far away. MJ and Harry then looked on, with Harry thinking it was awesome. However, Peter ran away, confused about what had just happened. ====Discovering his Powers==== [[File:Wall crawling.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter wall-crawling for the first time]] {{Quote|Go, web! Fly. Up, up and away, web. Shazam! Go! Go! Go, web, go!|Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} After the fight, Peter ran away, trying to get away from everything, then looked at his hand. Nearby, he noticed a spider crawling nearby, and looked at his hand again, noticing sharp stuff attached to his hand. He used this to climb the building, and then after realized he could climb buildings, he looked backwards excitedly, and started jumping from building to building, before getting to a building where he couldn't do it anymore. He then tried to find a hand gesture that shoots his web, before finding the right one as he couldn't command his webbing to shoot out but had to use two fingers on his palm to shoot the webbing out manually. He swung from that building to another, but he was unable to stop himself and hit his head on a billboard. ====Talking to MJ==== [[File:Peter and MJ talk 1.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter talks to Mary Jane about his plans after graduation]] {{Quote|So where are you going after you graduate?"
"I wanna move into the city. And hopefully, get a job as a photographer. Work my way through college. What about you?"
"Headed for the city too. Can't wait to get out of here.|[[Mary Jane Watson]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} Having come home late in the night, Parker realized the kitchen had already been painted, and found a note appeared telling him they had left meatloaf and vegetables in the fridge. However, he then saw MJ and her father [[Phillip Watson]] arguing with each other. After taking out the trash, MJ walked into her garden, frustrated, asking Peter if he knew what was going on. Peter then consoled MJ, telling her that even May and Ben could yell as well. After Peter apologized for what had happened at school that Mary Jane, the pair discussed their futures after their high school graduation, with Peter wanting to go to college and begin a photography career, and MJ wanting to act on stage, which Peter found to be perfect. [[File:Peter practising his webs.jpg|thumb|235x235px|Peter practicing his organic web-shooting]] However, Mary Jane left as she and Flash Thompson went for a ride with each other. After seeing MJ's reaction to Flash's new car, Peter then realized he could get a car for Mary Jane and began finding a car and potentially new ways to earn money. While looking for new cars, Parker found out he could earn money by wrestling, being able to win up to $3000 if he could win 3 minutes in the ring, with a colorful costume. Parker decided to work on a new suit and logo, basing it off a spider as he had the powers of one. A few days later, Parker decided to test his webbing capabilities, albeit in his own room. This lead to May asking what was going on, but Peter brushes it off as exercising, though he made a lot of webs in his room. ====Wrestling Match==== [[File:Wrestlingsuit.webp|left|thumb|250x250px|The Human Spider prepares to wrestle [[Bone Saw McGraw]]]] {{Quote|I can't help thinking about the last thing I said to him. He tried to tell me something important, and I threw it in his face.|Peter Parker to [[May Parker]]|Spider-Man (film)}} Sometime later, Parker decided to head off to the place where the wrestling match was being held. However, to disguise this, Parker told May and Ben he was actually going to the downtown library and planned to take the subway there. However, Ben offered to ride him there wanting to do some exercise. After arriving Ben talked with Peter about what had been going on with his life. Ben conversed with Peter about the fight at school with Flash, and then told him that with great power come great responsibility. Enraged with Ben's lecturing, Parker decided to leave the car and head to there himself. [[File:SpiderManWinsWrestlingMatch.jpg|thumb|250px|Spider-Man wins the wrestling match]] Peter then signed up to the NYWL wrestling match, despite the receptionist's reluctance at first, and then got ready for the fight. Initially naming him the Human Spider, the ring announcer decided to name him the Amazing Spider-Man, due to him not liking the name. After getting taunted by the crowd, Spider-Man and [[Bone Saw McGraw]] decided to fight each other in a cage match, much to Parker's frustration. He then dodged a lot of attacks, then climbed up the cage, deciding on taunting Bonesaw. This angered him, who tried reaching for Spider-Man but ended up falling down. Bonesaw then attempted to use a club and a chair to hit Spider-Man, but thanks to some repetitive kicks, Spider-Man was able to defeat him and win the fight. ==== Death Of Ben Parker ==== [[File:Spider-man 2002 robber escape.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter allows [[Dennis Carradine]] to escape]] {{Quote|I won the money, and the guy wouldn't pay me. Then he got robbed. The thief was running towards me. I could've stopped him, but I wanted revenge. I let him go. I let him get away.|Peter Parker to [[May Parker]]|Spider-Man 2}}Despite winning the fight, Spider-Man was only offered 100 dollars, despite the advertisement for it saying 3000. Parker then was told that he only got that much because he knocked him out so quickly. Bitter about this, Parker left, and decided not to stop a [[Dennis Carradine|robber]] who was stealing from the place. The person there then explained to him that he could have stopped him, which Spider-Man didn't care much about. [[File:Death of uncle ben.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter finds a dying Uncle Ben]] After winning the wrestling match, Parker waited for Ben, before noticing a large crowd surrounding a dead body, who was later identified as Ben Parker. Ben woke up, recognizing Peter and they held hands for a moment before Ben succumbed to his wounds. Enraged, Parker then decided to find the killer of Ben and maybe even kill him. ====Stopping Dennis Carradine==== [[File:Peter's tragic mistake.webp|left|thumb|253x253px|Peter reflects over his tragic mistake of losing Uncle Ben]] {{Quote|Don't hurt me. Just give me a chance. Just give me a chance!"
"What about my uncle? Did you give him a chance? Did you?|[[Dennis Carradine]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} After the tragedy that Peter faced, he put on the suit and decided to catch the killer of his uncle. He swung around town, before coming to an abandoned warehouse, finding the killer. After fighting with the killer, he pushed him to a wall, realizing that the killer was the same man whom he let escape the robbery. As the man was about to shoot Peter when he let his guard down, Peter injured him in self-defense. The man, frightened by Peter's superhuman strength, realizing he wasn't human and backed away before tripping over a pipe and fell out of the window, killing him upon impact. Peter managed to escape from the police before they could catch him. That night, Parker sat on top of the [[Chrysler Building]], reminiscing about what had happened. He then went home and embraced May, both being heartbroken about the loss of Ben. ====Graduation Ceremony==== [[File:Spider-man-graduation.webp|thumb|251x251px|Peter graduates from [[Midtown High School]], along with [[Harry Osborn|Harry]] and [[Mary Jane Watson|Mary Jane]]]] {{Quote|Peter, the science award. That's terrific."
"Yeah."
"I know this has been a difficult time for you, but I want you to try to enjoy this day. Commencement: The end of one thing, the start of something new.|[[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} After the tragic loss, Peter, alongside his peers, celebrated their high school graduation, with Parker receiving the science award. He then talked with Harry, who revealed that they both had a place to stay after graduating, before he talked to Norman. Norman then consoled Peter about Ben and told him that if he needed anything Norman would be right there for him. After getting home, Parker remembered the last moment he shared with Ben, and how he threw it away. May then told him that they both loved each other and that was what mattered. ===Becoming Spider-Man=== ====Post Graduation Life==== [[File:Peter fighting crime as Spider-Man.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter decides to use his powers for good and becomes the vigilante, Spider-Man]] {{Quote|♪Dresses like a spider He looks like a bug♪ ♪But we should all just give him One big hug♪ ♪Look out Here comes the Spider-Man♪|[[List of Minor Characters#Spider-Man|Subway Guitarist]]|Spider-Man (film)}} After high school, Parker found out he had kept his wrestling suit. However, after seeing his previous suit design, he perfected it with his original idea for the suit. Taking Ben's words into heart, Peter then decided to wear it to conceal his identity and fight crime as a vigilante/superhero known as the friendly neighborhood Spider-Man. He stopped various crimes, including a bodega robbery, a jeweler robbery, and so much more. However, some news outlets, including ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' believed him to be a menace to society and cannot get a photo of him swinging through the city, due to the building's high heights and people can only get a glimpse of him. ====Daily Bugle Freelancer==== [[File:Spider-man photos.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter selling photos of Spider-Man (himself) to the Bugle]] {{Quote|What other skills do you have Parker?"
"I was thinking of something in photography.|[[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} One day, while looking for a job, Parker noticed Mary Jane and talked with her. MJ then lied about her job, but was forced to reveal after her boss yelled at her that she worked for [[Moondance]]. MJ then revealed her and Harry had been going out together, much to Parker's frustration, and the two said their goodbyes to each other, leaving. Feeling down about this, Parker lied to Harry that he'd been fired by [[Curt Connors]], which Harry found funny as Parker was always late. He then talked to Norman, and found a newspaper from ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' wanting pictures taken of Spider-Man. Being Spider-Man, he stopped a gang assault with cameras around. Following the assault, Parker submitted them to the editor-in-chief of ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'', [[J. Jonah Jameson]]. Initially thinking of them as bad, he agreed to pay Parker 300 dollars, that being the standard fee for freelance photography. Parker then requested a job, which Jameson told him all he could offer was freelance. After having a small disagreement over Spider-Man being a hero or not, Jameson sent him to receptionist [[Betty Brant]] to ensure he'd get paid. ===[[Green Goblin's Rampage|Conflict with Green Goblin]]=== ====[[Attack on the World Festival|Attack on the World Unity Festival]]==== [[File:Spider-Man and Goblin first fight..png|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man battles the [[Green Goblin]]]] {{Quote|Wait. Who are you?"
"You know who I am."
"I do?."
"Your friendly neighborhood Spider-Man.|[[Mary Jane Watson]] and Spider-Man|Spider-Man (film)}} Peter attended the annual World Unity Fair organized by Oscorp, taking photos for the ''Bugle''. While there, he took a picture of the balcony, and noticed Harry with MJ there. He then noticed Harry attempting to make out with her, which MJ declined. Parker looked on in disappointment, but not after his spider-sense alerted him of a danger that was to come. Parker then saw the [[Green Goblin]] on his [[Goblin Glider|glider]] swooping in to cause the balcony to explode. After quickly webbing two people to safety, he then took of his clothes to reveal his suit. [[File:Mary Jane with Spider-Man.webp|thumb|250px|Spider-Man saves Mary Jane after the attack]] After putting on the suit, Spider-Man attacked Green Goblin to save Mary Jane, which caused a balloon to fall on a little kid called Billy. Spider-Man was able to save him before returning to fight the Green Goblin. After a while, Spider-Man destroyed the glider and stopped the Green Goblin from doing anything worse, and swung MJ all the way to a church. When MJ asked him who he was, he simply replied that he was the Friendly-Neighborhood Spider-Man, and swung away. ====Night at Harry's Loft==== {{Quote|What was [[Green Goblin|that thing]]?"
"I don't know. Whatever it is, somebody has to stop it.|[[New Goblin|Harry Osborn]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} After the fight at the World Unity Festival, Parker listened to Harry on the phone with MJ. After the call, Harry apologized to Peter knowing he had feelings for MJ, just Parker never acted. After, Harry decided to get some rest and talked to Peter about the Goblin. Peter revealed he didn't know who he was, but he needed to be stopped. ====[[Ambush on the Daily Bugle Office|Ambush at ''The Daily Bugle'']]==== [[File:SpideyWebsJJToTalkToGoblin.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Spider-Man stops Green Goblin's ambush]] {{Quote|Jameson, you slime! Who's the photographer who takes the pictures of Spider-Man?!"
"I don't know who he is, his stuff comes in the mail!|[[Green Goblin]] and [[J. Jonah Jameson]]|Spider-Man (film)}} The very next day, Parker submitted his pictures to Jameson, who yet again decided to criticize Spider-Man, while Parker defended him. Jameson then told Parker to leave and told him that he wasn't slandering Spider-Man. However, Jameson was attacked by Green Goblin, with Parker as a witness. As a result, Parker appeared as Spider-Man, and stopped the Goblin from doing worse to Jameson. However, Jameson attacked him, believing him to be working with the Goblin. As a result, Parker webbed J. Jonah Jameson's mouth and told him to keep quiet. However, the Goblin sprayed sleeping gas on Spider-Man and made him feel unconscious. [[File:2002.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man is confronted by [[Green Goblin]]]] After this, Parker was placed on a rooftop by the Green Goblin, and he preached to Spider-Man that despite his efforts for the people of [[New York City]], everyone would come to hate him soon enough. This rang in Parker's head, and he thought about it looking at a Daily Bugle newspaper advertising for Spider-Man's arrest. ====Rescuing MJ==== [[File:Spider-man and MJ.webp|left|thumb|249x249px|Spider-Man before his kiss with Mary Jane]] {{Quote|You have a knack for getting in trouble."
"You have a knack for saving my life.|Spider-Man and [[Mary Jane Watson]]|Spider-Man (film)}} One day, Parker met Mary Jane who had just arrived from a soap opera audition, and Parker asked how it went, knowing from May what had happened. Mary Jane, disappointed about the outcome said that she needed an acting license, and left to go out with Harry, leaving Parker disappointed. However, he sensed that MJ was being attacked by a criminal gang, and came to her rescue as Spider-Man, individually taking on each member of the gang. After, he realised he didn't have his mask on and quickly leaped to the top of a building to slip his mask on, saving his face from being seen. Spider-Man returned hanging upside down on his webbing and he talked with MJ, with whom he then shared an upside down kiss with. Peter, as Spider-Man silently swung away with Mary Jane smiling at him. ====Thanksgiving==== [[File:Duel at a burning building.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man is attacked by [[Green Goblin]]'s [[Razor Bats]]]] {{Quote|Sorry I'm late. It's a jungle out there. I had to beat an old lady with a stick to get these cranberries.|Peter Parker to [[May Parker]]|Spider-Man (film)}} The next day, the Green Goblin started a fire in a crowded apartment building to lure Spider-Man into a trap. Spider-Man saved a baby from the inferno but returned to the blaze at the call of one remaining victim. He then swung in to help the person that was still trapped, and looked around for her. He then tried comforting the old lady once he saw her, but she then revealed herself to be the Green Goblin, kicking Spider-Man a distance away. The Goblin then asked about the proposal if Spider-Man had decided a yes or no. Spider-Man, however, declined. This angered the Goblin who then threw [[Razor Bats]] at Spider-Man. After getting punched by the Green Goblin a few times, Spider-Man decided to block the razor bats with his hand, but ended up receiving a scar on his right hand. Despite the injury Peter defeated Goblin and then left. Peter then made it to their Thanksgiving Dinner, however, May, upon seeing Parker's sleeve, found a cut on his arm. Norman then left after seeing the scar. Despite Harry's efforts in trying to get him back Norman got angry and criticized MJ. After Harry got back, MJ criticized Norman and left the loft they lived in after Harry got mad at her. ====Visiting May==== [[File:WhenYouLookInHerEyes.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Peter speaks his true feelings towards Mary Jane]] {{Quote|You do too much. College, a job, all this time with me. You're not {{WPS|Superman|Superman|}}, you know.|[[May Parker]] to Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} A few days later, Parker found May hospitalized following the attack she faced from the [[Green Goblin]]. [[File:Aunt May and Peter.webp|thumb|250x250px|Peter keeping May company at the hospital]] That night Parker was woke up by May, who revealed she had heard the entire conversation between MJ and Parker. She told Parker to go home, but Parker refused and wanted her to be fine. She then recounted Parker first seeing MJ and thinking of her as an angel. Parker decided to call MJ, however the Green Goblin instead asked him to come out and fight. ====[[Battle at the Queensboro Bridge|Final Fight with the Green Goblin]]==== [[File:Pumpkin Bomb to the fac.jpg|thumb|250x250px|[[Battle at the Queensboro Bridge|Spider-Man's]] [[Spider-Man Suit|mask]] is blown up by a [[Pumpkin Bombs|Pumpkin Bomb]]|left]] {{Quote|Give me your hand. Believe in me as I believed in you. I've been like a father to you. Be a son to me now."
"I have a father. His name was [[Ben Parker]].|[[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn]] and Spider-Man|Spider-Man (film)}} After believing that MJ was in trouble, he swung to the scene as Spider-Man. However, the Goblin had not only MJ hostage but a few school children as well. The Green Goblin then asked Spider-Man to make his decision over who lives and dies. However, as Spider-Man was unable to make a decision, the Goblin pressured him by dropping both. Spider-Man then grabbed onto MJ and the wire holding onto the carriage. While being attacked by the Goblin, Spider-Man asked Mj to climb down, as he dropped the carriage onto a shipment. However, the Green Goblin then dragged Spider-Man to an abandoned warehouse. [[File:Battle at the Queensboro Bridge.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man is brutally punched by [[Green Goblin]]]] The Goblin then revealed his disappoint at Spider-Man of how he offered friendship but he refused. He ruthlessly beat up Peter, before revealing that he would finish off Mary Jane with a painful and slow death. The Green Goblin took a shiny green trident and attempted to kill Spider-Man, but what Green Goblin said about M.J enraged Spider-Man and caused him to regain his strength. When the Green Goblin tried to stab Spider-Man with his trident, Spider-Man grabbed it and in a fit of rage, threw him on to a brick wall. The Green Goblin got up, but Spider-Man used his webs to pull him to the ground and then brought down the brick wall on him. [[File:SpiderManSeesNormanDie.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Spider-Man witnesses Norman's death]] The Goblin got up, but was dazed. Spider-Man used his web to swing, grab Goblin and throw him against a wall. Spider-Man then began punching him, but stopped after Norman pleaded him to stop and took off his Green Goblin helmet to reveal himself. Peter was shocked to learn that the Goblin was really Harry's father. Norman tried asking for forgiveness, but while doing so, Goblin sent his [[Goblin Glider|glider]] at Spider-Man. While he dodged the glider, it ended up fatally impaling Osborn. Just as he was about to succumb to his wounds, he asked Parker to not tell Harry, and then died. Parker then returned Norman's dead body to his house, devastated at losing a father figure. However, Harry approached and saw Spider-Man next to Norman's lifeless body. He then grabbed a gun to kill Spider-Man with, only to see him gone. ===Later Life=== ====Norman Osborn's funeral==== [[File:Peter attends Norman's funeral.webp|thumb|255x255px|Peter attends Norman Osborn's funeral with Harry, MJ and May]] {{Quote|I'm so sorry, Harry. I know what it's like to lose a father."
"I didn't lose him. He was stolen from me. One day Spider-Man will pay. I swear on my father's grave, Spider-Man will pay.|[[New Goblin|Harry Osborn]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man (film)}} A few days later, Peter approached Harry at Norman's funeral. Parker then comforted Harry, telling him how he had also lost someone who was like a father to him. Harry revealed he thought that the Spider-Man killed him and believed that the Spider-Man shall pay. He then thanked Peter for being family to him and they both embraced, as Harry then left. Parker then decided to walk towards his uncle Ben's grave. [[File:PeterRejectsMJAtTheGraveyard.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Peter sadly rejects Mary Jane's love]] While standing next to his uncle's tombstone, he was approached by MJ, who comforted him over his loss of Ben, and then revealed that when she was struggling over the incident with the Goblin, she thought of Peter Parker to make her safe. She then admitted her love for Parker and they both shared a kiss. However, Parker decided to keep her out of his dangerous life and refused to start a relationship with her. However, he still told MJ he would be her friend. This made her tear up; however, she recognized that kiss from the one she shared with Spider-Man and began to wonder who he was. [[File:NYC Sam Raimi.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man swings around the city]] Parker, following this incident, then swung around the city to fight crime as Spider-Man, promising to never forget his uncles words: "With Great Power comes Great Responsibility." He decided to continue being Spider-Man to save the ones that he held dear. ====Struggles==== {{Quote|I'm Spider-Man, given a job to do. And I'm Peter Parker, and I, too, have a job.|Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} During the year [[2004]], which was two years after his conflict with the Green Goblin, Parker became a student of Doctor [[Curt Connors]] at [[Columbia University]]. He also found a job at [[Joe's Pizza]], working for [[Mr. Aziz]]. However, both began to lose faith in him due to his time as Spider-Man: for Connors, his grades and exhaustion were key factors in his lessons, and for Mr. Aziz it was Parker's lateness when it came to delivering pizzas. He also found an [[Peter Parker's Apartment|apartment]], however, his landlord [[Mr. Ditkovich]] often chastised him for not paying rent when it was due. This not only affected his professional life but his social life was affected as well, as he was unable to talk to Harry and MJ. ====Bad Birthday==== [[File:Peter pizza SM2.webp|left|thumb|265x265px|Peter with a job as a pizza delivery boy]] {{Quote|What's the occasion?"
"Really, Peter. It's your birthday!|Peter Parker and [[May Parker]]|Spider-Man 2}} One day, Parker was late yet again for work as he was distracted by a billboard advertising MJ's play. Eventually, Mr. Aziz stopped him and told him that 21 minutes before there was an order and he was defaulting on him to deliver in eight minutes due to Joe's Pizza having a 29-minute guarantee. However, Parker's duties as Spider-Man could not go without completing as he had to save two children whom he told not to play on the road. Eventually he was able to deliver the pizzas. However, they were not paid for by the receptionist because of him being late. Following the refused payment, Parker pleaded with Mr. Aziz for another chance, but instead he removed the Joe's Pizza sticker from his bike helmet. [[File:Daily Bugle office SM2.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter's photos are rejected by Jameson]] After his failed job, Parker was initially fired by Jameson for providing pictures of other things, before Parker gave him a picture of Spider-Man. Parker then showed the cheque given by Jameson to Betty Brant, however that did not cover the advance she had given previously. After feeling down, She told Parker he should chin up. However his day was met by more misery as he failed to attend Connor's class. Connors then reminded him of his paper on fusion, which Parker was planning to write on Otto Octavius. However, he was asked to get it done or Connors would fail him, Connors being a good friend of Octavius. [[File:Peter's birthday.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter having his 20th birthday with [[May Parker|May]], [[Harry Osborn|Harry]] and [[Mary Jane Watson|Mary Jane]]]] While feeling miserable about how bad his day was going on, Parker decided to visit May, only to see her, Harry and MJ. Due to how bad he was feeling, Parker forgot it was his birthday and celebrated with them. He talked to Harry, who revealed he was making a breakthrough on fusion by funding Doctor Octavius, who Parker was writing a paper on. Harry then surprised him with the fact that he could introduce him to Otto which delighted Parker. However, it then turned into a negative conversation after Harry talked about Parker's support for Spider-Man. Parker reminded him that he simply wanted to be friends with Harry. After the party, Parker found May's garnishment note and woke her up. She at first mistook him for Ben but then felt mournful at their loss. She then tried to cover up the garnishment note and offered money which Parker declined but was then told to take it by an agitated May. She then apologized as it was due to her mourning for Ben and said goodbye. After, Parker and MJ discussed outside that MJ was seeing a [[John Jameson|man]] whom she liked. Parker then promised to attend her play. However, Parker's Day would only get worse, as soon as he returned to his apartment his birthday money was taken away by landlord Mr. Ditkovich for rent purposes. Parker returned home and reflected on the day, before going to sleep. ====Meeting Doctor Octavius==== {{Quote|You're Connors' student. He tells me you're brilliant. He also tells me you're lazy."
"I'm trying to do better.|[[Doctor Octopus|Otto Octavius]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} Following his bad day, Parker woke up to use the bathroom only to find Mr. Ditkovich yet again asking for rent, which Parker responded by closing the door. [[File:Peter meets Otto.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter meets [[Doctor Octopus|Dr. Otto Octavius]]]] Later, Parker and Harry walked up towards Otto Octavius. Harry introduced them both to each other with Parker revealing he was actually writing a paper on Otto. Initially not wanting to talk to Parker, Otto decided to do so on Harry's proposal, stating that Oscorp pays the bills so it would be fair. Finally, Harry took off and Otto remembered Parker from Connors' talking's. He then told that due to his laziness, brilliance was not enough, and he should use intelligence for the good of mankind. Eventually, Otto then showed off his fusion reactor which impressed Parker. However, he then realized it could potentially destroy the entire city. [[File:Peter washes his Spider-Man Suit.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter washes his [[Spider-Man Suit]]]] However, Otto and his wife [[Rosalie Octavius|Rosie]] convinced him Otto would be the one to know. Parker displayed his knowledge of Bernoulli's curve of quickest descent which led Otto to praise Parker. Rosie then decided to ask if he had a love partner which Parker responded that he did not know. However, Otto insisted that love was not to be kept a secret and if he wanted to get a girlfriend, the best way was to read them poetry. Parker would take Otto's advice and practice it while getting his clothes cleaned. Later, Peter sat in a laundry store reading poetry while his clothes and secretly his Spider-Man Suit were being washed. When it was finished, Peter takes his suit out but noticed his suit had left marks on his other clothes. ====MJ's Play==== [[File:Peter takes the car.jpg|thumb|248x248px|Peter, as Spider-Man, drives the thieves' car to MJ's play]] {{Quote|John has seen my show five times. Harry has seen it twice. Aunt May has seen it. My sick mother got out of bed to see it. Even my father. He came backstage to borrow cash. But my best friend, who cares so much about me can't make an 8:00 curtain.|[[Mary Jane Watson]] to Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} After getting his clothes cleaned, Parker got ready to see MJ's play, [[wikipedia:The Importance of Being Earnest|''The Importance of Being Earnest'']]. As Parker collected his ticket, Parker evaded Mr. Diktovich's calls for rent yet again. Parker then bought flowers for her but was only able to get 3 of them. While content, Parker rode on his scooter to see MJ's play. However, Parker saw two criminals and dodged them just in time. This amazed two children nearby, both of whom asked him how he was able to do it, and Parker simply replied he worked out and had plenty of rest. After, Parker had to delay yet again due to the two criminals, and chased them around New York. After he successfully apprehended them, Parker arrived. However much to his frustration the usher would not allow him to sit because of him being late, which made Parker sit outside the theatre, listening to a violinist playing a tune of Spider-Man, much to his annoyance. After the play finished, Parker noticed Mary Jane and made plans to talk to her, but then saw Mary Jane with her boyfriend and realized it was too late. [[File:Spider-man web block 1.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man suffers a web block]] After, Parker tried to continue his war on crime as Spider-Man, however due to his bad luck, he suffered from a web block. This caused him to fall down and crash into some pipes. After realizing he could not shoot webs at that given moment, Spider-Man instead took the elevator to get downstairs, which was very awkward as he had to share the elevator with a guest who entered inside with his dog. [[File:Spider-Man takes the elevator.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man awkwardly uses an elevator]] The guest told Spider-Man his outfit looked cool, which Spider-Man thanked and admitted he made it when the guest asked him where he got it. After exchanging awkward words, Spider-Man left the elevator, changed back into Parker and walked back to his apartment, dragging his damaged moped along the sidewalk, dejected over his failure of attending Mary Jane's play. The next day, Parker used a public pay phone in order to call Mary Jane to apologize and confess what happened while he was on his way to her show, however he was unable to bring himself to speak the truth about him and Spider-Man. He eventually did; however, he gave up once realizing that MJ was not listening to his confession about him. ====Failed Fusion Reactor==== [[File:Spider-Man pulling the plugs.jpg|left|thumb|246x246px|Spider-Man stops the [[Fusion Reactor|fusion reactor]] by pulling the plugs]] {{Quote|What are you doing?"
"Pulling the plug.|Otto Octavius and Spider-Man|Spider-Man 2}} Following the disappointment surrounding Mary Jane's play, Parker alongside Harry attended Otto's test, where he presented his [[Fusion Reactor|fusion reactor]]. He then presented the tentacles, which would help because they were impervious to heat and magnetism. After, Otto turned the power on, causing the reactor to power. This impressed Parker and Harry, who clapped at the result. However, once more power was added the reaction eventually started to increase the magnetism and caused nearby objects to be attracted to the reactor. Parker soon ran away and suited up as Spider-Man. Spider-Man showed in time to save Harry. However, Harry reminded him he still hadn't forgiven him for what he had done to his father. Eventually upon learning that Spider-Man had showed up to remove the plug, Otto used his tentacles to attack Spider-Man believing the reactor would soon stabilize. Eventually, the Neural Inhibitor Chip fried up due to the reactor and Otto was left unconscious. This gave Spider-Man the advantage to pull the plug and shut the reactor down altogether. After this, Spider-Man left the building as Parker. === [[Doctor Octopus' Campaign|Conflict with Doctor Octopus]] === ====[[Robbery at the Manhattan Bank|Battle at the Bank]]==== [[File:Spider-Man vs Doc Ock.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man confronts [[Doctor Octopus]]]] {{Quote|You're getting on my nerves. Spider-Man"
"I have a knack for that.|[[Doctor Octopus]] to Spider-Man|Spider-Man 2}} After the failed demonstration, Brant found Parker the next day and brought him over to Jameson. Upset at Parker for not being able to provide a picture of the incident having been there himself, Jameson fired Parker. However upon remembering the Planetarium Party that would present J. Jonah Jameson's son John Jameson, he took back his decision so that Parker would be able to attend the ceremony for the purpose of taking pictures. When Parker asked if he could be paid in advance, however, Jameson simply laughed at this remark and asked him to leave the building. Following this incident, Parker accompanied May to the bank in order to get her financial struggles sorted. However they were unable to come to a compromise, despite Ben's life insurance and May's money from piano lessons. This upset May, who prompted to kick the bank attendant out of rage. However, they could not continue to negotiate any further because a cloaked figure later revealed to be Doctor Octopus decided to rob the bank. However this came to no avail because Spider-Man was able to bring Otto outside. Despite this, Otto grabbed May and prepared to climb up the building with her. [[File:Spider-Man faces Doc Ock.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man faces Doctor Octopus]] Spider-Man tried to save May, and was able to do so, until Doc Ock caught May. May was able to attack Octavius and cause herself to fall. Luckily Spider-Man was able to save her, which changed May's thoughts about him and led her to realize he was a hero. Spider-Man then left the scene, impressing everybody around. However, Doc Ock was still at large despite this incident. ====Night at the Planetarium Party==== [[File:Peter dejected.webp|thumb|250x250px|Peter attending the Planetarium party]] {{Quote|Ladies and gentlemen, I just want you all to know that the beautiful Miss Mary Jane Watson has just agreed to marry me.|[[John Jameson]]|Spider-Man 2}} After the fight at the bank, Parker continued his job at the Planetarium Party for the Bugle, however he then noticed Harry in a bad mood and went over to talk to him. However, Harry instead got angry over the failed fusion reactor and Parker's continued support for Spider-Man. Before things got any worse, however, Parker was picked from the crowd by Jameson and was asked to continuously take pictures of Jameson with several people. Eventually though, the rest of the crowd gazed in awe while noticing the astronaut [[John Jameson]] himself making an appearance. Parker then turned around only to notice MJ with him, making him realize they were in a relationship together. [[File:Peter and Harry at the party.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter being confronted by a drunk [[Harry Osborn|Harry]]]] Parker then tried to talk to MJ, and apologize for everything, but MJ simply stated they did not know each other and how it was painful to think about Parker. Parker then decided to follow Octavius' advice and read the poem ''The Song of Hiawatha'', but MJ decided to stop him. When Parker asked if he could get her a drink, MJ then reminded Parker of the times everyone else in her life attended the play, and left upset. After this, a disgusted Harry approached him, sick and tired of Parker for defending Spider-Man all the time and stealing MJ away from him. Harry then slapped Parker continuously, and left. Parker's life only got worse as it was revealed to everyone that John Jameson and MJ were now engaged. He was then asked by Jameson to continue taking pictures, which he did. ===Spider-Man No More=== ====Losing His Powers==== [[File:Spider-man web block 2.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man suffers another web block]] {{Quote|l'm Spider-Man. But I'm losing my powers. I'm climbing a wall, but I keep falling.|Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} That night while swinging through the city, Spider-Man tried to shoot another web but then realized he was beginning to have a web block again and fell into a dark alleyway. After trying to shoot another and failing, Spider-Man wondered what was going on. Parker then tried to see if he could still climb the walls, which was initially successful, however he still fell down onto the ground in a puddle. He then read another Daily Bugle newspaper which was in the trash nearby. However, it got blurry, and Parker retreated to his apartment, throwing the newspaper away. ====Giving Up==== [[File:Spider-Man No More.webp|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man: No More]] {{Quote|Take my hand, son."
"No, Uncle Ben. I'm just Peter Parker. I'm Spider-Man no more.|[[Ben Parker]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} After, Parker did an appointment with Doctor [[Wally Davis]] at [[Columbia University]]. However, there was nothing wrong with Parker physically, and instead there might have been something wrong inside his head. Parker then told Davis that his friend had a dream where he was Spider-Man and couldn't climb the walls. Davis then told him that he did not have to be doing this and that he had a choice in his life. Later that night after going to bed, Parker envisioned his Uncle Ben and told him he was in love with Mary Jane and that he wanted a life of his own rather than having to carry out all of Ben's wishes. Ben however reminded him that with great power comes great responsibility and asked him to continue his heroics. However Parker refused, stating that he was just Peter Parker and no longer Spider-Man. Parker then threw his suit in a nearby trash can that night and walked away. ====Improved Life==== [[File:Peter returns to normal life.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter returns to a normal life]] {{Quote|You look different."
"I shined my shoes, pressed my pants, did my homework.|[[Mary Jane Watson]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} After stopping his life as the masked vigilante, Parker surprisingly found himself happy, being able to fix his broken scooter in the car chase incident and also being able for him to focus on his studies, which Doctor Connors praised after initially being on the verge of failing him. However, this also turned out to become a problem as the crime rates unknowingly increased and despite knowing that nearby crimes were occurring, he instead ignored them believing he should not be responsible as Spider-Man anymore. He was also able to arrive at Mary Jane's play, which surprised her and they both mouthed a "hi" to each other before the play continued. After the play, Parker and MJ had a chat with each other, with MJ being surprised at Parker's appearance and she also realized there was something different about Parker. Parker then revealed to her that he had now been able to focus on his social life, being able to finally do his homework. However, when Parker suggested he could get her Chow Mein, MJ declined believing it was another attempt to get her in his life and reminded him she was getting married to John. Parker then tried reminding her of the time MJ told him she loved him, however MJ stated there was nothing to them and they were never in a relationship. MJ got frustrated and then left, reminding Parker she was getting married in a church. ====Revealing the Truth==== {{Quote|I'm responsible."
"For what?"
"For what happened to Uncle Ben.|Peter Parker and [[May Parker]]|Spider-Man 2}} One day, while walking down the street, Parker noticed some criminals beating down a young man nearby. This made Parker then look away and wonder about whether he had actually done the right choice of giving up his identity. [[File:Spider-man 2 Peter reveals the truth.webp|left|thumb|252x252px|Peter confesses to [[May Parker]] about [[Ben Parker|Uncle Ben]]'s death]] After, Parker alongside his aunt May, visited Ben's grave in order to pay their respects to the man they once lost with May placing flowers near the gravestone. After, they went to May's house, where she blamed herself for the loss of Ben and had tea with Parker. During tea, however, Parker told her she shouldn't blame herself. May then agreed and stated she thought she should have stopped Uncle Ben from driving him there. However, Parker instead revealed he didn't actually go to the library and that he went somewhere else to earn money. He then revealed he wanted revenge and how Uncle Ben died that night being the only person who did the right thing. May then left to go upstairs in shock. ====Fire Accident==== [[File:Peter burning building.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter bravely saves a little girl from a burning building, without his powers]] {{Quote|Am I not supposed to have what I want? What I need? What am I supposed to do?|Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} After this, Parker one day saw a newspaper made by The Daily Bugle which revealed that in the absence of Spider-Man, the crime rate in New York City had increased by 75%. However, Parker then heard some screaming nearby and heard that there was a young child stuck inside of the building, and that the building was covered in fire. Parker then rushed inside to save the child. However Parker had lost his powers therefore he could not break the door by itself and kicked it as a result. After dodging some falling rubble, Parker heard that the crying was coming from the closet of that room and opened it, rescuing the child. He then ran with her, but nearly fell down with the child. After placing the child nearby, Parker struggled to lift himself up due to having lost his strength. However the girl helped Parker up and they escaped the building, with Parker returning the girl back to her concerned parents. The fire and rescue department came and helped Parker to breathe properly. However, Peter overheard that one other person was trapped inside, and never made it out. That next day, Parker looked upon the city, wondering if he could have anything he wanted in the world. However, he was interrupted by [[Mr. Ditkovich]]'s daughter [[Ursula Ditkovich]], who apologized for not knocking the door and then closed it. She then knocked again, which Parker answered. She asked if Parker wanted chocolate cake and milk, which he accepted. After they ate together, Ursula collected their plates and told Parker there was a message from his aunt. [[File:Peter-listens May's speech.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter listens to May's heroic speech]] After, Parker visited his aunt May, who revealed that with the help of neighbor [[Henry Jackson]], she was getting her stuff ready to move out of her house despite being given a few more weeks to live and had found an apartment to stay at for the time being. Parker then tried to apologize but May revealed that to her it was forgotten at this point, and instead she was proud of Parker for being able to tell her the truth. Eventually Jackson greeted Parker who asked about Spider-Man, knowing he took pictures of him. Parker simply replied that Spider-Man quit, which Jackson felt upset about. May then agreed, stating Spider-Man was a hero, and that he set examples for everybody. She then told Parker she believed there was a hero in everybody, before asking Parker to lift the desk nearby. [[File:Peter my back.jpg|thumb|242x242px|Peter hurts his back after his failed attempt to return as Spider-Man]] Inspired by May's words, Parker decided to go on top of a building and decided he wanted to become Spider-Man again, with a strong focus on what he was trying to achieve. He then ran and jumped of the building ready to reach the next. After initially yelling out that he was finally Spider-Man and has his powers back, he then fell across some clothes and hit several cars. After, Parker held his back together as is was hit as a result of the fall. ====[[Attack on Mary Jane Watson]]==== [[File:Spider-Sense in SM2.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|[[Attack on Mary Jane Watson|Peter sensing]] [[Doctor Octopus|incoming danger]]]] {{Quote|What do you want?"
"I want you to find your friend Spider-Man. Tell him to meet me at the Westside Tower"
"I don't know where he is."
"Find him. Or I'll peel the flesh off her bones.|Peter Parker and [[Doctor Octopus]]|Spider-Man 2}} After this, Parker met Mary Jane Watson at a local café. She then thanked Parker for coming and stated the reason she asked him to come was because she believed she had to listen to what Parker had to say after her play was over. However, Parker refused to tell her, simply stating that he thought he could but couldn't. Watson then asked Parker if he loved her. Upon Parker saying no, Watson reached out of a kiss. However, Parker then felt an unknown presence nearby and grabbed Watson, ducking down. It was then revealed to be a car thrown by an unknown person, who later revealed himself to be [[Doctor Octopus]]. Doctor Octopus then used his tentacles to grab Parker close to him and asked him to find out where the Spider-Man was, or he would attack Watson. After Parker threatened Doc Ock, he was thrown into one of the café walls, with rubble falling over Parker. Octavius then grabbed Watson and went away. After, Parker was able to lift himself from the rubble and noticed the fact that he no longer needed glasses anymore and that his power was back. ===Spider-Man is Back=== ====Battle with Doctor Octopus==== [[File:Spider-man returns.webp|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man returns to face Doctor Octopus]] {{Quote|Where is she?"
"Oh, she'll be just fine. Let's talk.|Spider-Man and [[Doctor Octopus]]|Spider-Man 2}} After having found out his powers were back, Parker went to [[The Daily Bugle Tower]], where he took his suit during Jameson's speech on his regret of slandering Spider-Man. Jameson, upon seeing the suit had been taken, went back to his usual criticism of Spider-Man and a newspaper was published announcing this. Spider-Man then swung to the [[Westside Clocktower]]. After asking where MJ was, Doc Ock assured him she'd be fine and asked him to come closer. Spider-Man swung at him but Octavius used his tentacles to attack him. He then threw Spider-Man off and threw stuff at him, but Spider-Man was able to fight back. [[File:Spider man train fight.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man vs. [[Doctor Octopus]]]] As the fight hostile in mid air, Spider-Man eventually was able to pull Doctor Octopus down and they landed on a train. After they both latch onto the train, Spider-Man attempted to web up Doc Ock but was thrown towards him, leading Spider-Man was able to fight back. Doc Ock then knocked Spider-Man off the train and onto the streets. Spider-Man then came back ready to fight. After seeing Spider-Man back, Octavius retaliated by throwing random people off of the train. This caused Spider-Man to catch them and shoot webs to ensure their fall would not be painful. After this, Octavius then knocked out the captain of the train and pulled the plug after increasing it to the maximum speed. Octavius then left, reminding Spider-Man he had a train to catch. ====[[Duel on the Subway Train|Stopping The Train]]==== [[File:Peter stopping the train.png|thumb|250x250px|Peter stopping the train]] {{Quote|It's good to have you back, Spider-Man.|[[List of Minor Characters#Spider-Man 2|Boy with Mask]]|Spider-Man 2}} After Octavius left, Spider-Man took off his mask as a result of the flames and went to the front of the train, asking everybody to hang on. He then used his feet to stop the train, but his legs were injured as a result of this. When he was asked by the captain if he had some ideas, Spider-Man replied he did and used his webs to stick onto the nearby buildings and hold the train back. However, the walls of the buildings fell. Spider-Man then tried to do the exact same thing instead with more webs. This caused him to struggle and cut himself. Luckily, the train just about stopped. After this, Spider-Man felt exhausted. [[File:SpideyGetsHisMaskBack.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Spider-Man recovers from the incident]] Now passed out from exertion, Spider-Man was gently carried by the passengers across the train. Peter and Mary Jane mourned their friend's death as the sun rises. === Later Life as a Crime-Fighter === ==== Fixing his Relationship with MJ ==== [[File:Peter and MJ reconcile.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Peter and Mary Jane reconcile]] {{Quote|It took a while, but we made it work.|Peter Parker to [[w:c:amazingspiderman:Spider-Man|Peter Parker]]|wikipedia:Spider-Man: No Way Home}} Peter, Mary Jane, Gwen, Aunt May, Bernard, and several others attended Harry's funeral. Spider-Man also visited the Empire State Building, seeing that the view from there was sweet for him. === [https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Spider-Man_Multiversal_Crisis Spider-Man Multiversal Crisis] === ==== Brought to an Alternate Universe ==== {{Quote|I've been trying to find your friend ever since I got here. I just had this sense that he needs my help.|Peter Parker to [[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:marvel cinematic universe|Michelle Jones]] and [[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:marvel cinematic universe|Ned Leeds]]|wikipedia:Spider-Man: No Way Home}} On one day, during the year [[2024]], Peter found himself transported into an [https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe alternate universes' New York City] (Earth-199999). During his time there, Peter discovered that this universe's [https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Spider-Man Spider-Man's] identity had been outed and he was in trouble fighting some of the enemies Peter fought in the past. He also learned about this universe's Spider-Man's friends Ned Leeds and Michelle Jones through the media. Since then, Peter had been trying to find his alternate self, sensing that this universes' Spider-Man needed his help. Unknown to Peter, the [[Sandman]] and alternate versions of [[Green Goblin/Variant Green Goblin|Green Goblin]] and [[Doctor Octopus/Variant Doctor Octopus|Doctor Octopus]] (the latter two being versions of themselves prior to their deaths) from his [[Spider-Man Trilogy|universe]] (Earth-96283) were transported into this universe as well. ==== Meeting Ned Leeds and Michelle Jones ==== {{Quote|Wait, so you're Spider-Man too. Why didn't you just say that?"
"I generally don't go around advertising it. Kinda defeats the whole 'anonymous superhero' thing.|[[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Ned Leeds|Ned Leeds]] and Peter Parker|wikipedia:Spider-Man: No Way Home}}At night, he was walking around when an Inter-Dimensional Portal appeared before him. Peter stepped through the portal and found himself in the Leeds Residence, where Leeds and Jones were present. Leeds assumed he was a random person at first until he clarified his identity while greeting them and Leeds' grandmother. Peter then spotted [https://amazingspiderman.fandom.com/wiki/Spider-Man another Spider-Man] (another alternate version of Spider-Man from the [https://amazingspiderman.fandom.com/wiki/Timeline Earth-120703 universe]) and they both eyed each other, both sensing something, like two gunslingers sizing the other up. He realized that was not the Spider-Man native to Leeds' and Jones' universe before they quickly used their respective webs to shoot at the other. Both dodged their webs, and Peter shot his web at the other's Web-Shooter, before realizing the other person was another universe's Peter Parker and not the one he was looking for. After Leeds' grandmother politely asked the two web-slingers to clean the webs they shot, she went to bed and Peter approached Jones and Leeds. Peter told them that he had been trying to find their Spider-Man and sensed that he needed Peter's help. After Leeds and Jones explained that they did not know their Spider-Man's current location, Peter asked if there was a place that had meaning to him, and when he was about to continue speaking, the Earth-120703 Spider-Man said if there was place where he would get away from everything. There was a glance of acknowledgement between Peter and the Earth-120703 Spider-Man, with Peter saying that for him it was the top of the Chrysler Building and the Earth-120703 Spider-Man replying that for him it was the Empire State Building, believing that it had a better view, which Peter complimented that the view was sweet. This prompted Jones to realize that her Spider-Man was at Midtown School of Science and Technology. ==== Comforting his Counterpart ==== {{Quote|Sorry... about May.|Peter Parker to [[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Spider-Man|Spider-Man]]|wikipedia:Spider-Man: No Way Home}} The two Peters then learned that the younger Spider-Man's aunt May Parker was killed by Green Goblin and that the Macchina di Kadavus, which was in Jones' possession, could send the Multiversal travelers home, so they all went to the top of Midtown High, where the Earth-199999 Spider-Man was grieving. Spider-Man pulled off his mask, gasping for air as he watched as the sand continuing to fall away, to reveal Flint Marko, a man once again. He assured Marko that it was okay and that they were going to get him home, instructing him to stay inside. ===== Reuniting with Otto Octavius ===== {{Quote|Oh, it's good to see you, dear boy."

"You're all grown up. How are you?"
"Trying to do better.|Peter Parker and [[Doctor Octopus/Variant Doctor Octopus|Otto Octavius]]|wikipedia:Spider-Man: No Way Home}} Spider-Man then joined his other selves at the top of the crown as they fought Electro. When Octavius asked how he was, Spider-Man responded that he was trying to do better, echoing a conversation they had so many years before. An Interdimensional Portal appeared near the two, and Doctor Strange exited through it. Peter-One argued with Strange as Spider-Man and Peter-Three went next to Peter-One, surprising Strange. Having overheard their argument, a confused Spider-Man asked about Strange going to the Grand Canyon. The new spell resulted in the rest of the Multiversal travelers to be sent back to their home universes.
