Assuming Peter was pulled from the present time in his universe, he'd have been sent back to his original timeline of the Earth-96283 universe. ==Personality== {{Quote|You can't live in fear of what might happen. You care so much for others... you have to let someone care for you.|[[Mary Jane Watson]] to Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} Peter Parker, as Spider-Man, is shown to be a brave and righteous hero with an indomitable sense of justice and responsibility. Since the death of his wise and beloved Uncle Ben, Peter's vow of responsibility had led him to a path of heroism and humanitarian. To those in the authorities, Spider-Man was shown to be an outlaw and vigilante, someone just as dangerous as those he brought in. This was because the authorities mistrust Spider-Man at first, due to his vigilantism but he continued to fight crime in New York, in spite what everyone said about him. Over the years, Spider-Man gradually became respected by the authorities, those are the NYPD and NYFD, as well as the citizens of New York City, despite J. Jonah Jameson considering him as a masked menace on the Daily Bugle newspaper. Spider-Man is individualistic as he works by himself, this was caused when he was a social outcast in his youth. This had gradually lessened when he became best friends with Harry Osborn and then the boyfriend of Mary Jane. Peter was originally shy and awkward towards his life-long crush Mary Jane Watson, as he had a hard time talking to her and making conversations with her. Since he didn't want her to get hurt by his enemies, he wanted to be friends with her, but after Mary Jane discovers his superhero identity, she decides to be with him no matter what risks can be caused. He also has a sense of humor, sometimes cracking jokes in front of Bone Saw McGraw, Green Goblin, Doctor Octopus and Sandman. When the Venom Symbiote bonded to him, it unleashed his dark and vengeful side when he fought Sandman in the subway in a relentless way. The suit possessed him which made him beat up Harry at his penthouse after he blackmailed Mary Jane to "break up" with him and taunted him how his dad saw him as a failure, including throwing a pumpkin bomb at him, disfiguring the right side of Harry's face. Under the influence of the symbiote, he temporarily became arrogant and aggressive when he revealed Eddie Brock's fake photos to the Daily Bugle, getting him fired and unintentionally striking Mary Jane at the Jazz Club. He realized that the symbiote was controlling him, making his friends suffer the same way it did it for Peter. He did the right thing of rejecting and getting rid of the symbiote in a church, returning back to his true personality. After defeating Venom and Sandman, forgiving the latter, along with the unfortunate death of his best friend Harry Osborn, Peter realizes that whatever battle-raging inside himself and everyone he loves, he and his loved ones always had a choice. This was when Harry taught him and he will always choose to do what was right. Peter has a caring side when after much revelations with the black suit and having turbulent relationship, he eventually decided that it was time to mend his relationship with Mary Jane, which she returned as the two rekindled their romance and were back as a couple, though it took a while but they managed to make their relationship stable, despite the inconveniences of his dual life. Throughout his career as a crime-fighter, Peter became more mature and level-headed as he grew up, retaining his humbled nature, even after freeing himself from the black suit. He came to the realization that vengeance won't make him feel better and developed a strong sense of justice, especially a strict code against killing. Because of this, he is the most experienced of his alternative variants after years of crime-fighting. This was displayed when after he was transported to another reality and meeting his two alternative counterparts, he helped the younger Peter understand that vengeance was not what Aunt May would have wanted after her death at the hands of the Green Goblin, even stopping him from giving into his anger from impaling Osborn with his own glider, despite everything he done to both of them. When he met his two alternative counterparts, he bonded with them easily and became a big brother like figure to them, even giving them advice when they needed, especially when he helped his Earth-120703 counterpart accept the loss of Gwen Stacy and not to doubt himself. He was still quiet, but was willing to joke like his variants. He also learned from his Earth-199999 counterpart that there were other superheroes in his world, that he never knew, as no other superheroes were in his universe, which made him curious. Because he had spent years balancing his complicated life as both Spider-Man and as a civilian, having less resources and having no help from other heroes during his crime-fighting career, Peter was able to live an independent life as a superhero, by fighting crime alone. In addition, he, and his Earth-120703 counterpart, inspired his Earth-199999 counterpart to become more independent like him, always relying on Ben Parker's motto, rather than relying on other heroes. ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|You're an amazing creature, Spider-Man. You and I are not so different.|[[Green Goblin]] to Spider-Man|Spider-Man (film)}} *'''Spider-Physiology''': Spider-Man possesses his superhuman spider-powers and abilities after being bitten by a genetically engineered super spider, having gained the attributes of a spider such as superhuman strength, speed, agility, reflexes, stamina, durability, equilibrium, clinging ability to scale through surfaces and ceilings, and his heightened precognitive sixth sense to detect incoming threats known as "spider-sense". His body is muscular but extremely well proportioned with serious muscle definition. **'''Superhuman Strength''': Spider-Man possesses superhuman strength, which is the proportionate strength of a spider, allowing him to lift approximately 20 tons.[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wPtKrPP7CnY How Strong is the Raimiverse Spider-Man?] With a single punch, he was able to send [[Flash Thompson]] flying across a hallway, as well as puncture the roof of [[Dennis Carradine]]'s car. When he saved MJ in the rain, he was able to pull away several grown men at once with his webbing. He later held up a Roosevelt Island tram (filled with people) with one hand. His most impressive feat of strength was stopping a runaway train, even though it strained him painfully. He was able to hold up a piece of [[Doctor Octopus' Hideout]] wall and preventing it from crushing Mary Jane, even lifting it up and away. His strength was enhanced by the [[Venom]] symbiote, with a photograph depicting him holding up a motorcycle in each hand. During his final battle with Venom, he was able to repeatedly drive metal poles through concrete to surround the symbiote. His strength was sufficient enough to hold back his young counterpart from killing the Green Goblin with his glider, even when bending down on his knees. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Spider-Man's bones and muscles are incredible dense to support his strength. As such, he can survive falling from incredible heights, as seen when he was experiencing a web block and he stopped producing webs while swinging, or when he jumped off a building and landed on a car. He even survived a Pumpkin Bomb detonating in his face, whereas Harry was disfigured by one. He also withstood Harry stabbing him with a knife. He even survived from being stabbed by the Green Goblin, noting he had been stabbed multiple times before. His fingers are as hard as steel. **'''Superhuman Agility''': Spider-Man is exceptionally agile and acrobatic in his movements, surpassing that of an Olympic gymnast; he can leap 40 feet into the air without feeling bound by gravity. He moves with a lithe, skittering grace that seems barely human. He was able to perform a double backflip over Flash's crony, who was attempting to tackle him. he can practically surf rooftops.''[[Spider-Man (novel)]]'' Chapter 7 He was also able to backflip over the Goblin's glider to avoid being impaled. He could save MJ when [[Otto Octavius]] threw a car at them.[[File:Peter running.jpg|thumb|250px|Peter running very fast]] **'''Superhuman Speed''': Spider-Man can move at incredible superhuman speeds, having been able to catch up to his [[Midtown High|school]] bus on foot. In combat, he can move with blinding speed, as seen during his duel with [[Flash Thompson]]. He can travel great distances in even shorter amounts of time by swinging on his webs; he was once tasked by Aziz with traveling 42 blocks in less than eight minutes. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Spider-Man can exert himself for extended periods of time without tiring. While sprinting used to leave him breathless, his breathing and heartbeat are now slow, steady, and sure, as if he does not even strain himself. His heartbeat had remained steady no matter what sort of physical exertions to which he had been subjecting himself. Even after stopping a train and pushing his muscular endurance, he fainted from exhaustion only for a few minutes. He fought both [[Venom]] and [[Sandman]] before Harry arrived to help. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Spider-Man has great reflexes, as stated by [[Mary Jane Watson|MJ Watson]]. The speed of his reflexes allow him to dodge any attack, even when he is far away. **'''Superhuman Senses''': Spider-Man could see perfectly with his glasses off following the [[Spiders|spider]] bite. His vision is even better than 20/20, enabling him to see 20 yards away.''[[Spider-Man (novel)]]'' Chapter 5 He can move with grace to easily balance a tray full of food.''[[Spider-Man (novel)]]'' Chapter 6 He said his eyesight would make a hawk jealous. He can see incredibly far, fast, and clear, being able to see the end of a train track from a long distance.[[File:SpideySensesTrainInDanger.jpg|thumb|250px|Peter senses upcoming danger for the train]] ***'''[[Spider-Sense]]''': Spider-Man has an early awareness of danger, which is a sixth sense or a "spider-sense". He first felt the sensation when [[Mary Jane Watson|Mary Jane]] was about to slip and fall in the cafeteria, but he caught her and her tray of food without spilling anything. He was able to avoid [[Flash Thompson|Flash]]'s punch and subsequent attacks from others. During the [[Attack on the World Festival|attack at the World Festival]], he was able to sense incoming danger, which was revealed to be the [[Green Goblin]], flying down on his glider, minutes before he arrived. Throughout his final battle with the Green Goblin, he sensed the [[Goblin Glider]] behind him when he realized his enemy was preparing to impale him from behind, making him dodge out of the way, just in time before it could strike him, even though it impaled the Goblin instead. After he lost his powers, his spider-sense immediately returned as it warned him when a car was thrown through the window of the cafe by [[Doctor Octopus]], that he and Mary Jane were in, allowing him to dodge the car and protect Mary Jane from the impact. He also sensed the end of the train tracks when the train's brakes were destroyed, making him realize he had to stop the train before it could plunge off the track. Spider-Man was able to sense [[Gwen Stacy]] falling from a building when a crane malfunctioned and caused her to fall. When he bonded to the alien symbiote, he sensed a [[Pumpkin Bombs|Pumpkin Bomb]] being thrown at him by Harry and he dodged it. His spider-sense was strong enough to alert him about his Earth-199999 counterpart in danger when he learned that he was incriminated by Mysterio for the crimes he didn't commit and knowing he needed his help. However, he was unable to sense Venom, due to him bypassing his senses, making him to get attacked by him without warning.[[File:Spider-Man Wall-Crawling.webp|thumb|260x260px|Spider-Man wall-crawling on the Statue of Liberty, while in Earth-199999]] **'''Wall-Crawling''': Spider-Man can crawl up walls because of the barbed hairs on his body. He can also stick to walls and ceilings no matter how rough or slippery. **'''Organic Web Generation''': Spider-Man developed spinnerets in his forearms to secrete webbing with great tensile strength. He can shoot webs for considerable distances in order to swing on them for rapid transportation, as well as apply them in combat. However, an existential crisis can take a toll on his ability to shoot webs and all his other powers. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor:''' Spider-Man has an enhanced healing ability, allowing him to heal higher than normal humans, he stopped using his glasses because his eyes were healed to perfection, and his eyesight was now superhuman. He healed from the spider bite in one day, During his second battle with the Green Goblin, he had a bloody cut on his wrist, which eventually healed in a few hours. During his final battle with Green Goblin, he was fatigued and injured then regain his strength, stamina, and senses, and made a full recovery from all of his injuries in one hour. He recovered after he woke up after being unconscious after his second battle with Doc Ock, and healed from minor injuries. After his final battle with Doctor Octopus, he healed the stab wound by Harry to the abdoman with a little blood to come out without scaring. His regenerative power enhanced his metabolism, immune system and health. ===Former Powers=== {{Quote|[[Venom|This]] is something else.|Spider-Man|Spider-Man 3}} *'''Physical Augmentation''': Spider-Man's physical spider-prowess was augmented even further by the [[Venom]] symbiote. However, he relayed to [[Eddie Brock]] that while the power felt good, he lost sight of who he was. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|Did {{WPS|Thomas Edison|Edison}} sleep before he turned on the light? Did {{WPS|Guglielmo Marconi|Marconi}} sleep before he turned on the radio? Did {{WPS|Ludwig van Beethoven|Beethoven}} sleep before he wrote the {{WPS|Symphony No. 5 (Beethoven)|5th}}?"
"Did {{WPS|Johann Bernoulli|Bernoulli}} sleep before he found the curves of quickest descent?|[[Doctor Octopus|Otto Octavius]] and Peter Parker|Spider-Man 2}} *'''Genius-Level Intelligence''': Peter Parker was descrbed as the living personification of intelligence.''[[Spider-Man (novel)]]'' Chapter 2 He is a scientific genius, as [[Harry Osborn]] described him as a prize-winning "science whiz" to his father [[Green Goblin|Norman]], whose research on technology he has completely read and written a paper on; the senior Osborn was impressed. He also referred to him as "the biggest brain in [[Midtown High School|school]]" to [[Flash Thompson]]. During the field trip to [[Columbia University]], he demonstrated familiarity with the most advanced electron microscope on the eastern seaboard, as well as knowledge in entomology, telling Harry that some spiders change colors to blend into their environment as a defense mechanism. He has been called "brilliant" by Aunt May and [[Curt Connors]], and a "genius" by Harry and [[Gwen Stacy]]. Peter was also capable of noticing potential flaws in [[Otto Octavius]]'s [[Fusion Reactor]], which was eventually proven to be right. Parker is also a skilled artist and can play the piano, and he was in the debating team and chess club in high school. **'''Master Scientist:''' Peter is a highly talented scientist, being an expert in various fields of science, ranging from quantum physics, biophysics and new physics. He helped Harry pass chemistry. He was able to answer questions correctly during his time at [[Columbia University]], by looking at the blackboards in his biophysics classes with [[Curt Connors]]. During his time with his two alternative counterparts, he was able to create an Anti-Goblin Serum that would cure a [[Green Goblin/Variant Green Goblin|variant]] of [[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn]] from the [[Goblin Formula]], having stated he had been thinking about it for a long time. {{Quote|Peter's been with us for years. He's done a great job.|[[Robbie Robertson]] to [[J. Jonah Jameson]]|Spider-Man 3}} *'''Skilled Photographer:''' Peter is a very skilled photographer. He worked as a freelance photographer for the [[The Daily Bugle]] for years until he took [[Eddie Brock]]'s staff job. {{Quote|I didn't start that fight. I told you that."
"Well, you sure as hell finished it.|Peter Parker and [[Ben Parker]]|Spider-Man (film)}} [[File:Spider-ManWebsDocOckArms.webp|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man fights and webs Doctor Octopus' arms at once]] *'''Master Combatant''': Spider-Man became a naturally skilled crime-fighter after gaining his powers. His fighting style is very gymnastic and includes martial art skills and hand-to-hand combat techniques from {{WPS|Boxing}}, {{WPS|Wing Chun}}, {{WPS|Karate}}, {{WPS|Taekwondo}}, {{WPS|Capoeira}}, {{WPS|Judo}}, {{WPS|Krav Maga}}, {{WPS|Muay Thai}}, {{WPS|Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu}}, and {{WPS|Professional Wrestling}}.[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8SuEth91C1k&t=319s How many fighting styles does Spider-Man know in the Spider-Man Trilogy?] As such, he was able to effortlessly defeat [[Flash Thompson]] and pin the reigning [[Bone Saw McGraw]] in two minutes. The wrestling league promoter also said he could have taken [[Dennis Carradine]] apart. Following his uncle's death, Spider-Man beat the robber up, disarming him of his weapons. He was able to defeat groups of criminals on his own with ease, he was able to defeat the [[Green Goblin]]. He has managed to take fights with the dangerous [[Doctor Octopus]], despite being briefly overpowered by his [[Doctor Octopus' Tentacles|tentacles]]. Spider-Man also was able to pick up a fight with [[Sandman]] and later, [[Venom]], though he was overpowered by the latter, due to Venom having the same powers he does. His fighting skills had improved as he grew up and he proved to be a superior fighter compared to his Earth-120703 and Earth-199999 counterparts. {{Quote|With my aim, I couldn't catch an elephant, much less a cold. It was like trying to precisely aim water while using a broken hose. But after a while, I got the hang of it. It just went where I aimed it. I can't tell you how thrilled I was. I started shooting right and left, like a gunslinger taking out bushwhackers. And it wasn't enough to just "shoot" stuff with the webs. I snagged stuff with pinpoint precision and pulled on it, sending it flying across the room. I was totally into it, not thinking about what I was doing. I don't know, maybe I kind of blanked out or something.|Peter Parker in Chapter 7|Spider-Man (novel)}} *'''Master Marksman''': Spider-Man can accurately fire his webbing to ensnare objects or attack his enemies. He is very precise, as he shot both [[Green Goblin]] and [[J. Jonah Jameson]] in the face. He can also fire concussive balls of webbing, as seen against a car full of criminals, [[Doctor Octopus]] and [[Harry Osborn]]. *[[File:Three Spider-Men swinging.webp|thumb|274x274px|Spider-Man web-swinging acrobatically with his counterparts]]'''Master Acrobat''': Spider-Man is a master of acrobatics and gymnastics, which he is able to use while navigating his environment with a combination of his superhuman agility and web-swinging. He is able to perform acrobatic stunts when web-swinging through New York City without physically straining his muscles. *'''Stealth Expert''': As Spider-Man, Peter moves so silently that the absence of sound that would have prompted any onlooker that maybe he was not there at all.''[[Spider-Man (novel)]]'' Prologue *'''Indomitable Will:''' Spider-Man has a strong, powerful, and unstoppable force of will, spirit, determination free of all evil and temptation. He has an absolute refusal to kill or give up, making him a formidable opponent against his enemies, he overcomes fear at almost anyway. Due to this, he struggled to balance his life as a student and a superhero. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''[[Pumpkin Bombs]]''': Spider-Man used these from Harry's glider so that he could defeat the [[Venom]] symbiote. He also used this when he was possessed by the Venom Symbiote, it was thrown at him by Harry, and swung it back at him, which disfigured Harry. ===Other Equipment=== *[[File:Spider-Man raimiverse.webp|thumb|250px|Spider-Man's Suit]]'''[[Spider-Man Suit]]''': In order to hide his identity, Peter wore a spandex suit with a mask consisting white, triangular eye-lenses to conceal his identity as Spider-Man. The suit featured red and black coloring in order to match the colors of the [[Spiders|spider]] that bit him. It also featured silver webbing across the suit. He also has spare suits in case any of them get damaged or ripped. **'''Human Spider Suit''': When signing up for a wrestling match, Peter wore this suit with a red ski-mask and a red jumper with webbing and a spider logo in the middle. Peter then wore this suit to chase his uncle's killer. Later, Peter took elements from this suit and incorporated it into his Spider-Man suit. **'''Original Suit''': This suit was used when Peter Parker first became Spider-Man. It featured silver webbing, a lighter red and blue color scheme with a shorter logo. The suit was damaged during a battle with the [[Green Goblin]]. **'''Updated Suit''': This was created after the fight with the Green Goblin. The suit features a larger logo and a darker red and blue color scheme. Peter used this suit to fight [[Doctor Octopus]], [[Eddie Brock]]/[[Venom]] and the [[Sandman]]. He continued to use this suit for his further experiences as Spider-Man. ===Former Equipment=== *'''Symbiote Suit''': Once bonded to the [[Venom]] symbiote, Peter wore an all-black suit which maintained the silver webbing, and also had a curved spider symbol. He rejected it when he discovered its true nature. ==Facilities== *'''[[Parker Residence|Parker Residence:]]''' When his parents died at the age of four, Peter moved into his aunt and uncle's house in Forest Hills, Queens, New York. He lived in the Parker Residence since he was young and loved his [[May Parker|Aunt May]] and [[Ben Parker|Uncle Ben]], looking up to them as if they were his parents. At aged 17, Peter returned to the residence after his school field trip to [[Columbia University]]'s labs where he was bitten by a [[Spiders|genetically engineered super spider]]. After he graduated from high school and entered college, Peter found out the residence was facing foreclosure until May decided to move into a [[May Parker's Apartment|small apartment]] in the city. *'''[[Peter Parker and Harry Osborn's Apartment|Peter Parker and Harry Osborn's Apartment:]]''' Following his high school graduation, Peter moved into this apartment and became roommates with his best friend [[Harry Osborn]], since his father, [[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn]], owned the apartments as he was the CEO of [[Oscorp]]. During Thanksgiving, Peter and Harry let their families and friends attend their Thanksgiving dinner at their apartment. *'''[[Peter Parker's Apartment|Peter Parker's Apartment:]]''' After moving out of his previous shared apartment with Harry, Peter moved into a rundown apartment near the [[Empire State Building]] where he lived on the top floor, next to his landlord, [[Mr. Ditkovich]] and his daughter, [[Ursula Ditkovich|Ursula]]. Peter also made his apartment his secret headquarters when he was operating as Spider-Man, having installed a police scanner to inform him about any crimes that have been occuring around [[New York City]]. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== * [[Mary Parker]] - Mother † * [[Richard Parker]] - Father † *[[May Parker]] - Aunt *[[Ben Parker]] - Uncle † ===Allies=== *[[Midtown High School]] - Former School *[[Mary Jane Watson]] - Girlfriend *[[Harry Osborn]] - Best Friend and Temporary Enemy † *[[The Daily Bugle]] **[[J. Jonah Jameson]] - Employer **[[Robbie Robertson]] **[[Betty Brant]] **[[Ted Hoffman]] *[[Columbia University]] **[[Curt Connors]] *[[Joe's Pizza]] **[[Rahi Aziz]] - Former Employer *[[John Jameson]] *[[Doctor Octopus|Otto Octavius/Doctor Octopus]] - Idol and Former Archenemy † *[[Rosalie Octavius]] † *[[Raymond]] *[[Sandman|Flint Marko/Sandman]] - Former Enemy turned Friend *[[Green Goblin/Variant Green Goblin|Norman Osborn/Green Goblin]] - Friend and Former Attempted Killer *[[Doctor Octopus/Variant Doctor Octopus|Otto Octavius/Doctor Octopus]] - Idol and Former Archenemy *[[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Ned Leeds|Ned Leeds]] *[[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Michelle Jones|Michelle Jones]] *[[w:c:amazingspiderman:Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] - Alternative Self and Friend *[[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] - Alternative Self and Friend *[[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Doctor Strange|Stephen Strange/Doctor Strange]] *[[w:c:amazingspiderman:Electro/Variant Electro|Maxwell Dillon/Electro]] - Attempted Killer *[[w:c:amazingspiderman:Lizard/Variant Lizard|Curt Connors/Lizard]] - Attempted Killer{{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn/Green Goblin]] - Friend turned Archenemy, Attempted Killer † *[[Eddie Brock|Eddie Brock/Venom]] - Arch-rival, Attempted Killer † *[[Venom|Venom Symbiote]] - Suit turned Archenemy and Victim † *[[Dennis Carradine]] - Attempted Killer † {{Col-end}} ==Trivia== *In order to distinguish him from his other two multiverse counterparts, Marvel.com has named him the '''Friendly-Neighborhood Spider-Man'''. * In Spider-Man: No Way Home Peter Parker is forty years old. *Parker's safe space is the top of the [[Chrysler Building]]. *Parker's soft drink of choice is Dr. Pepper. *Parker's favorite flavor of pie is cherry.''[[Spider-Man (novel)]]'' Chapter 7 *There are differences between this incarnation of Spider-Man and his comic book counterpart. In his origin, Peter wrestled to win money to impress Mary Jane with a car in the first film. In the original source material, he volunteered to wrestle a man named Crusher Hogan to test his powers, another alteration was Flint Marko was responsible for his uncle's death in the third film while in the comic book, it was a burglar backstage of a TV show that Spider-Man was winning money from as well as becoming Spider-Man after graduating. The other main differences that have been shown are falling in love with Mary Jane Watson since childhood in the first film while in comics, he didn't love her until around his college years and after the death of Gwen Stacy. **In the comics, Spider-Man used mechanical web-shooters with artificial webbing he designed himself, this version of Spider-Man has the ability to shoot webbing out of his wrist. **Originally, Peter was going to make his own web-shooters, however, the concept was scrapped. **The Web-Shooters were changed due to Sam Raimi's decision of making it more realistic since a high school student could not have created something of that. *This iteration of Spider-Man takes inspirations of many ideas of the Ultimate Comics instead such as Peter meeting Harry and Mary Jane in high school and the Spider being genetical alternated instead of radioactive. **Funny enough but Tobey Maguire and Sam Raimi appears on an arc of the Ultimate Spider-Man Comic Book where Maguire protocolized an In-Universe movie based on Spider-Man with the main villain being Doctor Octopus. * Unlike the comic book version of Spider-Man's suit, Sam Raimi's Spider-Man suit has a silver raised webbing pattern and more triangular eye lenses. *It was revealed that Tobey was going to come back as Spider-Man again if ''Spider-Man 4'' wasn't canceled in 2010, but it didn't work out with Sam Raimi. Tobey Maguire's participation was in doubt at one point because he was suffering severe back pains. Jake Gyllenhaal, was lined up to play Spider-Man and had already begun preparation, but Maguire decided to take part after all. However, according to the DVD commentary, the "My back!" joke after Peter falls from the roof was purely coincidental, as it was written into the script before Maguire's problem arose. Gyllenhaal would go on to play Spider-Man's enemy Mysterio fifteen years later in Spider-Man: Far from Home (2019). *''Spider-Man 2'' filmed the train scene in Chicago to incorporate it in New York because of the Directors decision. *The studio had expressed interest in actors Leonardo DiCaprio, Edward Furlong, Jude Law, Freddie Prinze Jr., Chris Klein, Wes Bentley, and Heath Ledger for Spider-Man. Furlong had been considered by James Cameron for the role in 1996, while Sam Raimi joked of Prince that "[he] won't even be allowed to buy a ticket to see this film." Also, actors Scott Speedman, Jay Rodan, and James Franco were involved in screen tests for the lead role with Franco later being cast as Harry Osborn. *Tobey Maguire said he had never read a Spider-Man comic book, but took the role because he liked the script. *One of the chief difficulties that Tobey Maguire experienced in the now-famous upside-down kissing scene was that his sinuses kept filling up with water as it was performed in driving rain. By signing on for two sequels, Tobey Maguire secured himself a paycheck of $26 million. *To acquire his bumped-up physique, Tobey Maguire went through a strict five-month regimen of exercise, weight training, and martial arts six times a week, as well as eating a high protein meal four to six times a day. *When Sam Raimi first offered to cast Tobey Maguire as Spider-Man, the studio was initially very reluctant. That was until they saw Maguire's test and they saw that the actor had bulked up for the role. *Spider-Man's webbing in the film is made out of foam materials and fishing line. It was also enhanced with CGI. *Tobey Maguire had to have his Spider-Man outfit slightly remodeled as the original design had not made any allowances for when the actor needed a bathroom break. A vent was added to enable him to perform without having to take the entire costume off. *During the fight between the Green Goblin and Spider-Man near the end, Willem Dafoe accidentally clipped Tobey Maguire on the chin with one blow. *When used in the trailer, the shot of Peter doing a long back-flip onto a car hood was digitally altered to put him in his Spider-Man outfit instead of his wrestling outfit. *When two studio executives were shown shots of the computer-generated Spider-Man, they believed it was Tobey Maguire performing the stunts. * Both Tobey Maguire and Willem Dafoe incorporated a Yoga technique called Ashtanga into their training regimens for this film. "Ashtanga" translates into English as "eight-legged," like a spider. * Zach Hudson, the stunt double for Tobey Maguire, fractured his leg after a stunt went wrong and he slammed into a brick wall. *Tobey Maguire was approached for the film largely as a result of his performance in ''The Cider House Rules'' (1999). Sam Raimi felt that Maguire's performance in the film embodied much of the character and personality traits he was looking for in Peter/Spider-Man. *Josh Hartnett turned down the role of Peter Parker/Spider-Man. Frankie Muniz and Topher Grace were considered for the role of Peter Parker/Spider-Man. Grace would later play Venom in the third film. Leonardo DiCaprio was considered for the role of Peter Parker/Spider-Man, he later appeared with Tobey Maguire in ''The Great Gatsby'' (2013). * One of the scenes that Tobey Maguire performed for his screen test was the final one in which Peter Parker rejects Mary Jane. *The train fight between Doc Ock and Spider-Man was the idea of director Sam Raimi and the first major sequence to be filmed. *Tobey Maguire's participation was in doubt at one point because he was suffering severe back pains. Jake Gyllenhaal was lined up to play Spider-Man and had already begun preparation, but Maguire decided to take part after all. However, according to the DVD commentary, the "My back!" joke after Peter falls from the roof was purely coincidental, as it was written into the script before Maguire's problem arose. Gyllenhaal would go on to play Spider-Man's enemy Mysterio fifteen years later in ''Spider-Man: Far from Home'' (2019). *When Peter dumps his Spider-Man suit in a garbage can, it's an exacting homage to a panel from "Spider-Man No More", Issue #50 of ''The Amazing Spider-Man''. As in the film, the outfit is found and brought to J. Jonah Jameson, then reclaimed by Spider-Man who leaves a note like the one in the movie. *Peter rents above a TV repair shop. He doesn't live with Harry anymore since Norman's death, and Harry moved back into his father's townhouse. Harry's obsession with Spider-Man was too much for Peter to bear. This is the reason why he gets annoyed with Harry at his birthday party. *There are several visual references to ''Spider-Man'' (2002) in the sequel: **Peter running into a burning building to save a child. **Peter running across a rooftop before trying (and failing) to shoot his web. **Peter putting out the trash in Aunt May's back yard and speaking to Mary Jane. **Spider-Man lying incapacitated on the sofa of his enemy. **POV of Peter Parker looking through his glasses and seeing a blurred image. *It has been recently announced that some actors from previous Spider-Man films portrayed their respective characters again in the third Spider-Man film set within the Marvel Cinematic Universe, with Tobey Maguire reprising his role as Spider-Man. **Interestingly, ''The Incredible Hulk'' director Louis Leterrier originally intended to have Tobey Maguire cameo as Peter Parker in the movie. ==Behind the Scenes== *Spider-Man in the Spider-Man Trilogy is the first live-action incarnation of the character which does not use web shooters, instead using organic webbing. **Earlier concepts show Parker wearing web shooters, but this was cut. *Spider-Man in the [[Spider-Man Trilogy]] is the second live-action incarnation of the character following an incarnation portrayed by {{WPS|Nicholas Hammond}}, and precedes incarnations portrayed by {{WPS|Andrew Garfield}} and {{WPS|Tom Holland}}. *Maguire was excited to portray a much darker version of Peter Parker/Spider-Man in ''Spider-Man 3''.[https://web.archive.org/web/20070422201143/http://www.ew.com/ew/article/0%2C%2C20035326_3%2C00.html World Wide Web | EW.com] *Spider-Man makes appearance in ''{{WPS|Spider-Man: No Way Home}}'' where he is transported into the universe as a result of the spell by Doctor Strange. However, he is sent back towards the end of the movie. Additionally, Spider-Man appears alongside his two successors in the film. *According to the novelization, Peter was born in August. However, it is unknown what exact birthdate he was born in the films. ==References== {{Gallery|Spider-Man/Gallery}} {{Wq|Spider-Man}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{WP}} *{{WP|Peter Parker (Sam Raimi film series)}} *{{Marvel}} *[https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Friendly_Neighborhood_Spider-Man Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man] on [https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe_Wiki Marvel Cinematic Universe Wikia] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Heroes]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Midtown High Students]] [[Category:Columbia University Students]] [[Category:The Daily Bugle Employees]] [[Category:Vigilantes]] [[Category:Joe's Pizza Employees]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:Enhanced Individuals]] [[Category:Spider-Man (film) Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man 2 Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man 3 Characters]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Blue Eyes]] [[Category:Spider-Man: No Way Home Characters]] [[Category:Love Exalted]]
